blocks.content: Along the coast of the Kingdom of Dionas, numerous artillery installations could be seen.
Beyond the dense jungle lay the royal castle, and colorful birds flew in the sky.
Shirone, aboard Arachne's ship, initially prohibited the refugees from approaching.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: There might be cannon fire. Let's wait here while I go ashore.
The sound of a cannon echoed.
Shirone's party turned to see that what had been fired into the sky was a firework filled with confetti.
Soon, lively music played, and a fast boat approached quickly.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Stop!
The knights of Arachne aboard the fast boat spoke with the pirates and returned.

blocks.speaker: Knight
blocks.content: King Kalt of Dionas has sent a welcome letter. He invites us to the royal castle.
Cairns read the letter and crumpled the paper.

blocks.speaker: Cairns
blocks.content: Hmph! How shameless. After sending his brother to attack us, now he invites us in?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Perhaps he thought fighting at sea was his only chance. Anyway, we're going. What about Arachne?

blocks.speaker: Cairns
blocks.content: …Of course, we must go. But the ship will not dock. We can't bring the treasure into unknown territory.
Thus, a small team consisting of Cairns, his attendants, Shirone, and the apostles was formed.

blocks.speaker: Langi
blocks.content: I'll go too.

blocks.speaker: Cairns
blocks.content: Let me come along. It will help. First impressions are important.

blocks.speaker: Cairns
blocks.content: You?
Cairns was displeased, as she was the most important asset in the negotiations.

blocks.speaker: Langi
blocks.content: This is enemy territory. If you're in danger, it could put us at a disadvantage diplomatically.

blocks.speaker: Langi
blocks.content: I don't feel safer here. The safest place is…
Langi hugged Shirone's arm.

blocks.speaker: Langi
blocks.content: Right here, isn't it?
Cairns was at a loss for words.

blocks.speaker: Cairns
blocks.content: Is that alright?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Let's go together. We have Jakra in our custody. I'm not sure how valuable he is as a hostage, but…
Thus, eleven people boarded Dionas's fast boat and landed.
As they crossed the jungle, they saw a complex path connected by planks between the trees.
There were countless small outposts, and an unmistakable sense of hostility emanated from within.
Poine smiled.

blocks.speaker: Poine
blocks.content: Their hostility is almost embarrassing. Not that it would have gone unnoticed.
The rough path required Langi and Cairns to receive magical assistance several times to reach the castle.

blocks.speaker: Langi
blocks.content: This is the royal castle?
It was a brick building that might have been fashionable 300 years ago, and its size was comparable to a monastery in the central continent.

blocks.speaker: Poine
blocks.content: His Majesty is a humble man. He seeks a simple life without greed for wealth or power.
Poine laughed at the envoy's explanation.

blocks.speaker: Poine
blocks.content: Such a man orders the kidnapping of a young maiden? Not that a pirate's word means much.

blocks.speaker: Cairns
blocks.content: Do not insult His Majesty. Trying to read his thoughts will only lead to death.
The truth of this was evident from the terrified Jakra.
When the castle gates opened, pirates lined up along a red carpet.
It was indeed a display befitting the wealth of a great pirate, but Shirone focused on the man before him.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Kalt.
A man with disheveled red hair raised his palm to greet them.

blocks.speaker: Kalt
blocks.content: Welcome, travelers, to the sanctuary of peace.
While most people spoke in whole tones, Kalt's speech was mostly in half-tones.

blocks.speaker: Kalt
blocks.content: Worry not. As long as I am here, nothing will harm you.
His strange intonation was unsettling, artistic, and repulsive.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I am one of the Five Great Stars of the Ivory Tower.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Your brother, Jakra, attacked our ship. You need to explain.
Kalt glanced indifferently at his brother.

blocks.speaker: Kalt
blocks.content: You wouldn't claim ignorance, would you? He confessed everything last night.
Mm! Mm!
Jakra, breaking even the mental technique, shook his head, clearly not in his right mind.

blocks.speaker: Kalt
blocks.content: Yes, I sent my brother. To welcome my soul mate, Langi.
Kalt placed his hand on his chest and bowed.

blocks.speaker: Kalt
blocks.content: Thank you for bringing her here. You are still as beautiful as ever, Langi.
Langi felt a shiver run through her body.

blocks.speaker: Langi
blocks.content: Oh, I see you've trimmed your bangs. About 2 centimeters, no, 2.3 centimeters.
While Langi didn't mind a meticulous man, this was too much.

blocks.speaker: Langi
blocks.content: Your eyelashes. The thirteenth one is bent by about 0.7 degrees. Where did the fine hair on your wrist, 7.3 centimeters up, go? It's my favorite.
It was mentally nauseating.

blocks.speaker: Kalt
blocks.content: There's no need to fear. I won't touch you. I don't need to.
Kalt's eyelids began to twitch.

blocks.speaker: Kalt
blocks.content: Because I already feel it.
He spoke slowly, almost crawling out the words, drooling as he continued.

blocks.speaker: Kalt
blocks.content: I feel everything. Your body, your reactions, your thoughts… Ah, I just became one with you. Integration. It feels amazing.
Langi's face was tearful.

blocks.speaker: Langi
blocks.content: I hate this. I'd rather die.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Integration.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What are you?
The ability to perceive everything about Langi could be explained by the effects of an electromagnetic field.
And it reminded Shirone of a special sensory concept he knew.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ultima System.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The ultimate sense might be electrical.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The Ultima System he had accepted in Babel was also electrical.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If the universe was born from electricity, it's not strange for the ultimate sense to be electrical.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The unique, first one.

blocks.speaker: Kalt
blocks.content: Who am I?

blocks.speaker: Kalt
blocks.content: One who feels the waves of the world. One who is nothing. One who could be a god or mere compost.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Similar to when I absorbed the Ultima System in Babel. Impossible to explain with just the five senses, but…

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He could feel it.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If that immense feeling had dominated him for over ten years, it was understandable that he wasn't sane.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You don't seem to know, so I'll tell you. Kalt, you're sick. You need treatment.

blocks.speaker: Kalt
blocks.content: Treatment? I'm sick?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes. It's because of the OOPArts. I don't know how you got it, but it made you this way. I can help. Where is it?
He spoke sincerely.
It was why Kalt, who had ignored everyone else, was silent.

blocks.speaker: Kalt
blocks.content: I'm sick…
The sensation conveyed by Shirone's voice, felt as a wave, was true.
The pirates raised their weapons.

blocks.speaker: Pirate
blocks.content: Your Majesty! Don't be fooled! They want to steal the OOPArts from us!
In truth, they had never seen the OOPArts.
Since Kalt's personality changed 13 years ago, everything had gone well.

blocks.speaker: Kalt
blocks.content: OOPArts? Ah, you mean Maika.
Kalt reached out to the air with a distant gaze.

blocks.speaker: Kalt
blocks.content: It's a beautiful place. Can you see it? It's here, floating in the air.
He snapped back to reality and looked at Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Kalt
blocks.content: Do you want to see it? Shall I show you?
This time, Poine intervened.

blocks.speaker: Poine
blocks.content: Messiah, it's dangerous. We shouldn't act without a complete analysis.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It was a reasonable statement, but Shirone's frequency was already resonating with Kalt.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You can go to Maika?

blocks.speaker: Kalt
blocks.content: No, not go. They're calling. Yes, it feels like they're calling.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: They.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Words spoken with the five senses could become entirely new at the level of the eleven senses…

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Alright. I need to see for myself.
If Kalt had returned safely, there was no reason he couldn't go.

blocks.speaker: Kalt
blocks.content: Come this way.
Kalt pressed his temples, and blue electricity began to swirl in his eyes.
After a moment, he stuck out his tongue, revealing a thin, rectangular metal plate.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What's that?
The Arachne officials murmured, but Shirone wasn't particularly surprised.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Living beings can't create machines. Therefore, quantum phenomena. Maybe Kalt…

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Had already become an OOPArts?

blocks.speaker: Kalt
blocks.content: Messiah, this is dangerous. Please don't implant foreign objects in your body.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's fine. It's just a material made with the five senses. I can control it.
Shirone examined the metal plate Kalt handed him.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What do I do now?

blocks.speaker: Kalt
blocks.content: I don't know. Just… stick it in your head? That's what I did with my subordinates.
Shirone pressed the chip behind his ear.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It won't go in.
It seemed he had to pierce the bone, but could that be called a function?

blocks.speaker: Kalt
blocks.content: It's okay. Trust me.
Kalt's hazy eyes didn't inspire confidence, but Shirone applied pressure with his fingers.
Hup!
Did he really pierce the bone?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: That didn't happen, but electricity flowed through the metal plate, invading Shirone's mind.
Gasp!
Shirone's eyes flashed blue, and a magnetic field enveloped him.

blocks.speaker: Kaos
blocks.content: Messiah!
Kaos and Poine, who had leaped forward, felt a familiar shock and grimaced.
Grrr!
If it progressed further, their memories would be damaged, but the two apostles reached out until the end.

blocks.speaker: Kaos
blocks.content: Me…ssi…ah…
Just before Kaos's hand, wrapped in electricity, touched Shirone, Kalt muttered.

blocks.speaker: Kalt
blocks.content: Meet them.
Ahhh!
As the two apostles risked their lives, Shirone's body vanished in an instant.

blocks.speaker: Kaos
blocks.content: Where? Where did he go?
Kaos, eyes tightly shut, pointed his sword at Kalt.

blocks.speaker: Kaos
blocks.content: First and last chance. Where did the Messiah go? If you don't want to die, you'd better tell me.

blocks.speaker: Kalt
blocks.content: I don't know.
Kalt had a somewhat sullen expression.

blocks.speaker: Kalt
blocks.content: Perhaps... a terrible truth.
The ultra-ancient civilization of Maika.
Gasp! Gasp!
Shirone waited until the blue tint enveloping his retinas disappeared.
As his vision cleared, he looked around.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Maika.
He was certain because it was unlike any place in Omega.
The city, built in terraces, had numerous buildings like a real civilization.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's not a civilization.
But upon entering the buildings, there were no traces of anyone having lived there.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: This is... a kind of device.
The entire city felt like a machine built for a single purpose.
At the center, two horn-like curved towers stood 20 meters high.
…I don't understand.
The towers were inscribed with characters, but none could be deciphered.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The Ultima System doesn't work.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Faced with the definitive evidence that these signals didn't exist in the universe, Shirone shuddered.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If there is an outside world…

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The closest imagination would be a higher layer implemented based on the mechanism of the void.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But that doesn't matter.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No matter how far you descend from reality, the end of imagination is still reality.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No one knows until they go there themselves.
Shirone took a deep breath.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Focus. What's there isn't important. What's truly important is…

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Why does it exist?
Like a distant memory, the city began to shake, and electricity flowed through the ground.
Ugh!
As he felt the current and gritted his teeth, an opaque barrier formed between the two towers.
Inside the barrier, a giant eye was staring at Shirone.
